title: citrus macroptera var. combara
intro: Citrus macroptera var. combara, also known as the Maypuri or Maypuri orange, is a rare and endemic species of citrus tree native to the Philippines. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, diarrhea, and rheumatism, and is also believed to have antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves and fruits of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
